stem.rawText:
Use the trapezium rule with 3 strips to estimate
                                         2
                                            2 log10 x dx
                                          1
                                          2


               √
                  6

solution.rawText:
The interval has width 2 − 12 = 32 , so each strip will have width 21 . The boundaries of the
three strips will be at 12 , 1, 32 and 2. The trapezium rule then gives the estimate (writing
f(x) = 2 log10 x):
                         Z 2
                              f(x) dx ≈ 21 × 21 f( 12 ) + 2f(1) + 2f( 23 ) + f(2)
                                                                                  
                           1
                           2


We can now work out the individual function values:

                               f( 12 ) = 2 log10 12 = −2 log10 2
                               f(1) = 2 log10 1 = 0
                               f( 32 ) = 2 log10 32 = 2 log10 3 − 2 log10 2
                               f(2) = 2 log10 2

Therefore we get the approximation
          Z 2
              f(x) dx ≈ 12 × 12 −2 log10 2 + 2 × 0 + 2(2 log10 3 − 2 log10 2) + 2 log10 2
                                                                                          
             1
             2

                       = 41 (4 log10 3 − 4 log10 2)
                       = log10 3 − log10 2
                       = log10 32

which is option B.
